issues.description:
Establish a foundational skill that encodes standardized code review methodology, persona-based analysis, and consistent output formatting — serving as the guidance layer for all pull request review workflows.

### Acceptance Criteria:

* Defines three review modes (simple, focused, complex) with clear scope and depth guidance
* Defines four reviewer personas with independent checklists and verdict framing
* Produces GitHub-compatible markdown output (references formatting rules)
* Uses progressive disclosure via references/ directory pattern
* Skill is tested and validated in a real PR review scenario

issues.summary: Automated RUNBOOK.md compliance check on pull requests
issues.description:
We need automated check on every pull request that verifies repository's `RUNBOOK.md` meets freshness and formatting requirements, so that runbooks don't silently go stale and on-call engineers can trust the documented procedures.

The check runs as a reusable GitHub Actions workflow (`check-runbook.yml`) backed by a shell script (`scripts/check-runbook.sh`). It is informational — it posts a sticky PR comment with findings but does not block merging or deployment.

### Acceptance Criteria:

1.  Skip if no runbook — If `RUNBOOK.md` does not exist, the check passes with no comment posted.
2. No template placeholders — The check fails if the file still contains `YYYY-MM-DD` placeholder text from the `adgear/repo-template`.
3. Parseable review dates — The check fails if "Last reviewed" or "Next review due" dates cannot be parsed as valid `YYYY-MM-DD` values.
4. Review date matches git history — The check fails if "Last reviewed" is earlier than or ahead of the last commit date that modified `RUNBOOK.md`.
5. Staleness detection — The check fails if "Next review due" is in the past relative to today's date.
6. PR comment on failure — If any of the above checks fail, a sticky comment is posted listing the issues with a link to the runbook template. If a previous failure comment exists and the check now passes, it is removed.
7.  Org-wide required workflow — The workflow is registered as a required workflow at the organization level and runs automatically on all repositories.

issues.summary: AI skill for IaC engineering standards
issues.description:
Establish a foundational skill that encodes organizational Terraform conventions, compliance requirements, and engineering best practices — serving as the guidance layer for all infrastructure provisioning skills.

###   Acceptance Criteria:

* Adapts JPL/NASA Power of 10 rules to Terraform context
* Adapts Terraform best practices (<custom data-type="smartlink" data-id="id-0">http://terraform-best-practices.com</custom> )
* Adapts custom Samsung Ads rules (allowed providers/modules list from TFE)
* Follows AWS resource naming conventions (references aws-naming skill)
* Defines naming convention rules for Terraform resources (locals, variables, outputs, modules)
* Skill is tested and validated in a real provisioning scenario

issues.summary: Migrate EMR cluster into TFE on stage environment
issues.description:
Need to migrate old terraform code into TFE version. In this task I will be dealing with EMR clusters in the STAGE environment.  
  
Edit: The migration turned out to be more complicated than expected. So we had to rebuild the module from scratch, incorporating the fixes that came out during the failure BIM-583. Below is a description of all the changes.  

```
PART 3 - Building EMR Clusters in TFE

  Module: modules/emr_new/ (new module, replaces modules/emr/)
